definition: G7 fiscal divergence is at its widest since post-WWII: U.S. at -6.5 to -7.0% of GDP, Euro Area ~-3%, Canada ~-1.5 to -2.0%, Germany ~-2%, Japan ~-4% at 250%+ debt/GDP. Three historical episodes of major divergence produced divergent dollar and cross-asset outcomes depending on the monetary-fiscal policy mix.
evidence: IMF Fiscal Monitor data, CBO projections
1981-85: dollar up 50% (Volcker tightening); 2001-04: dollar down 35% (Greenspan easing); 2017-19: initial strength then fading
Current mix (Fed on hold, wide deficits) maps closer to 2001-04 dollar-weak pattern
open_questions: Dollar direction: gradual weakening or sustained strength via rate differentials?
How long does reserve currency buffering delay external adjustment (historical: 2-4 years)?
Does European defense spending ramp alter the divergence trajectory?
